Another AEO Agency Launched This Month Selling What Google Just Said Doesn't Work

AI-written, human-reviewed 2026-07-14 by IsraeliLeads

On July 3, Coozmoo Digital Solutions announced it had launched Answer Engine Optimization and Generative Engine Optimization services meant to get brands "visible, cited, and recommended" inside ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini, Copilot, and Claude. The pitch, distributed through a paid press release, leans on a line from co-founder Arvind Waghela: search hasn't disappeared, it has "moved inside the answer." The service is powered by a tracking dashboard called RankRabbit AI, which the company says monitors where and how a brand appears across AI platforms.

We covered the other side of this story on this site less than two weeks ago. Google quietly updated its guide to generative AI search on June 29 and named the exact tactics the AEO industry has been selling: llms.txt files, chunking content into AI-friendly pieces, rewriting copy specifically for AI, chasing inauthentic mentions, over-investing in structured data. Google said plainly that none of it moves visibility in Search or its AI features.

Coozmoo's launch doesn't specify which tactics it's selling beyond "own the answer" framing and a measurement product. That's worth sitting with. The measurement piece is the legitimate half of this pitch. Knowing whether your brand gets mentioned when someone asks an AI assistant a category question, without your brand name in the prompt, is a real and useful thing to track. Most companies have no visibility into that at all right now.

The manipulation half is shakier. Citation is not the same as trust, and it's not the same as cause and effect. An AI system can cite a page because it retrieved it during that specific query, not because the model actually relied on it to form the recommendation. It can mention a brand without linking to it, or lean on model priors that have nothing to do with anything a vendor optimized. Paying an agency to "get you cited" assumes a level of control over model behavior that nobody selling these services can actually demonstrate.

The sensible version of AEO looks almost boring by comparison: accurate product descriptions that don't contradict each other across your site, partner listings, and press releases. Documentation that's current instead of three product cycles behind. Reviews and third-party coverage an AI system can actually retrieve and corroborate. That's information architecture, not sorcery, and it's mostly work you can do yourself before paying a retainer to have someone else do it slower.

If you want an honest read on where you currently stand, before signing anything, check it yourself first. If you want to check your own visibility across ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Gemini without committing to a monthly retainer, an AI-search-native research tool like Searchable ↗ will get you a similar visibility read to what an agency dashboard promises, and you can decide from there whether the manipulation side of the pitch is worth paying for at all.

The honest takeaway from Coozmoo's launch isn't that AEO is fake. It's that the category is filling up fast with vendors selling the same acronym at different price points, and the businesses that actually see results will be the ones with clean, consistent, well-documented information regardless of who they pay to package it.
